replacement_target: later post-baseline AIDE roadmap ordering artifact or explicit replacement checkpoint only

recommended_next_prompt.timing: after the canonical playable baseline exists
recommended_next_prompt.purpose: Review post-baseline evidence against the frozen operating and extraction boundaries, then admit or refuse a first post-baseline extraction-review tranche.

pre_baseline_xstack_aide_work.rule: No new XStack/AIDE prompt should run before the playable baseline exists.
use_existing_series_during_baseline: Use X-7 as the repo-operating law for build, validation, playtest, session, directory-authority, task-sizing, and commit rules.
Use X-0 and X-1 to keep scope, ownership, and mixed-surface cautions explicit while fixing baseline blockers.
Use X-2 through X-6 as contract vocabulary only when baseline work needs task, evidence, review, policy, capability, or adapter language.
Use X-8 to avoid accidental extraction or migration when touching portable-looking but still Dominium-owned surfaces.

prompt_order.notes: Resolve startup, session, and loopback-path blockers using the audit-backed baseline-first plan.
Prove one repeatable repo-local baseline path with the canonical bundle, lock, session template, and loopback-authoritative mode.

prompt_order.prompt_or_band: fresh FAST validation and agreed smoke slice
prompt_order.notes: Re-run FAST and the narrowed smoke gate against the assembled baseline.
Only after the baseline is real should post-baseline extraction review planning resume.

why_this_order_is_best: The XStack/AIDE narrowing-and-contract phase is already complete in its intended scope.
The repo gets more immediate value from baseline hardening than from more extraction planning today.
Post-baseline evidence will better distinguish truly extractable substrate from still-Dominium-owned integration glue.
This ordering prevents the repo from mistaking contract freezes for permission to start migration, platformization, or a second competing architecture track.
post_baseline_entry_expectations: one canonical repo-local playable path exists
session materialization works on the canonical layout
one repeatable start or status or save-load-resume slice exists
a fresh successful FAST validation run exists
the X-8 extraction prerequisites can be re-scored against live post-baseline evidence
